TESTING
18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers   n 3-15
For Service Technician Use Only
Wash Pump
This test will check the wiring to the wash/rinse motor and the 
motor itself. The following items are part of the wash/rinse 
motor circuit.  

n Harness/Connection

n Wash Pump & Capacitor

n Control Board
Test Procedure
1.	 Check the tub and sump for anything that may be 
impeding water flow. Inspect and if necessary clean the 
coarse-filter system. Also, inspect spray arm water nozzles 
and clean if needed. 
2.	 Check the wash pump and electrical connections by 
performing the “Service Mode Test” on page 2-3. The 
following steps assume that this step was unsuccessful. 
3.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 
4.	 Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to 
access control board.  
5.	 Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade 
screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove 
drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components.
6.	 Unplug connector CON1 and CON2 from control board. 
7.	 Check the wash pump—using an ohmmeter, measure the 
resistance across the high and low pump coils as shown in 
the following chart. 
MOTOR COIL
PINOUTS
RESISTANCE
High Coil
CON2, Pin 2 to CON1, pin 1
44-54 ohms
Low Coil
CON2, Pin 3 to CON1, pin 1
69-87 ohms
¾
¾
If the resistance is within range, the wash pump and 
harness are good. Go to step 8. 
¾
¾
If far outside the range, replace the wash pump.  
¾
¾
If an open circuit is detected, check connections and 
harness continuity between control and wash pump. If 
good, replace the wash pump.  
8.	 Reconnect CON1 and CON2 to control board. 
9. 	Test Wash Pump High Coil—Set voltmeter to AC and 
connect leads to CON2, pin 2 and CON1, pin 1 on the 
control board. Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power.  
10. 	Start the Service Mode Test and at the proper step 
measure for AC out of the control between CON2-2 and 
CON1-1. (Refer to Wash Motor Strip Circuit below.)	
¾
¾
If no AC voltage is measured, replace the control board 
and retest. 
¾
¾
If 120 VAC is measured and wash pump is not running, 
go to step 12. 
¾
¾
If 120 VAC is measured and wash pump is running, go 
to step 14. 
11.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.  Repeat steps 9 
and 10 for the Wash Pump Low Coil. Use pinouts CON2-3 
and CON1-1. 
12.  Test the wash pump capacitor. NOTE: A faulty capacitor 
may cause the motor to “hum,” not start, or start slowly.
a. 	Discharge the capacitor by touching the leads of a 
20,000 Ω resistor to the two terminals.
b. 	Disconnect the wires from the capacitor terminals.
c. 	With an ohmmeter, measure across the terminals and 
note reading. 
¾
¾
If a steady increase in resistance is noted, capacitor is 
good. Replace wash motor assembly and retest.  
¾
¾
If the capacitor is either shorted or open, replace 
capacitor. and retest. 
13.	 If the preceding steps did not correct the wash/rinse 
motor problem, replace the control board. 
14.  Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 
15.	 Reassemble all parts and panels. 
16.	 Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and run “Service 
Mode Test” to verify repair.  
DANGER
Electrical Shock Hazard
Only authorized technicians should perform 
diagnostic voltage measurements.
After performing voltage measurements, 
disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in 
death or electrical shock.
STRIP CIRCUIT - WASH PUMP
Figure 2 - Wash Pump Strip Circuit
CONTROL BOARD
CONTROL BOARD
L1
CON1-2
N

HIGH: ~ 49 Ω

text: Testing for Service Technician Use Only: Drain Pump

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-16  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
For Service Technician Use Only
Drain Pump
The drain pump is located under the tub and operates on 120 
VAC. It is energized for the first 60 seconds of a new cycle and 
90 seconds after the wash pump shuts down to remove any 
water in the dishwasher sump. The drain pump forces water 
out of the drain line. A check valve flapper on the drain pump 
prevents the dirty water from reentering the sump.
This test will check the wiring to the drain pump and the drain 
pump itself. The following items are part of the drain pump 
circuit.  

n Harness/Connection

n Drain Pump

n Control Board
Test Procedure
1.	 Verify that drain hose or drain path is not obstructed. 
Check for blockage from sump check valve to customer’s 
plumbing. Check for plugged hoses, plugged garbage 
disposal, or disposal plug not knocked out. Repair as 
needed. 
2.	 Check the drain pump and electrical connections by 
performing “Service Mode Test” on page 2-3. The 
following steps assume that this step was unsuccessful. 
3.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 
4.	 Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to 
access control board. 
5.	 Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade 
screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove 
drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components. 	
6.	 Unplug connector CON2 from control board. 
7.	 Check the drain motor—using an ohmmeter, measure the 
resistance between CON2-1 and CON1-1. 
¾
¾
If the resistance is between 25-35 ohms, the drain 
motor and harness are good. Go to step 8. 
¾
¾
If far outside the range, replace the drain pump.  
¾
¾
If an open circuit is detected, check connections and 
harness continuity between control and drain pump. If 
good, replace the drain pump.  
8.	 Reconnect CON2 to control board.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-16  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
For Service Technician Use Only
Drain Pump
The drain pump is located under the tub and operates on 120 
VAC. It is energized for the first 60 seconds of a new cycle and 
90 seconds after the wash pump shuts down to remove any 
water in the dishwasher sump. The drain pump forces water 
out of the drain line. A check valve flapper on the drain pump 
prevents the dirty water from reentering the sump.
This test will check the wiring to the drain pump and the drain 
pump itself. The following items are part of the drain pump 
circuit.  

n Harness/Connection

n Drain Pump

n Control Board
Test Procedure
1.	 Verify that drain hose or drain path is not obstructed. 
Check for blockage from sump check valve to customer’s 
plumbing. Check for plugged hoses, plugged garbage 
disposal, or disposal plug not knocked out. Repair as 
needed. 
2.	 Check the drain pump and electrical connections by 
performing “Service Mode Test” on page 2-3. The 
following steps assume that this step was unsuccessful. 
3.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 
4.	 Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to 
access control board. 
5.	 Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade 
screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove 
drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components. 	
6.	 Unplug connector CON2 from control board. 
7.	 Check the drain motor—using an ohmmeter, measure the 
resistance between CON2-1 and CON1-1. 
¾
¾
If the resistance is between 25-35 ohms, the drain 
motor and harness are good. Go to step 8. 
¾
¾
If far outside the range, replace the drain pump.  
¾
¾
If an open circuit is detected, check connections and 
harness continuity between control and drain pump. If 
good, replace the drain pump.  
8.	 Reconnect CON2 to control board. 
9. 	Set voltmeter to AC. Connect red lead to CON2-1 and 
black lead to and CON1-1 on the control board. Plug in 
dishwasher or reconnect power.  
10. 	Start the Service Mode Test and at step-5 measure for AC 
out of the control between CON2-1 and CON1-1. 
	
IMPORTANT: The Drain Motor must be connected to the 
control board to measure voltage accurately!
¾
¾
If no AC voltage is measured, replace the control board 
and retest. 
¾
¾
If 120 VAC is measured and drain pump is running, go 
to step 11. 
11.  Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power. 
12.	 Reassemble all parts and panels. 
13.	 Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and run Service 
Mode to verify repair.  
DANGER
Electrical Shock Hazard
Only authorized technicians should perform 
diagnostic voltage measurements.
After performing voltage measurements, 
disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to follow these instructions can result in 
death or electrical shock.

TESTING
18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers   n 3-17
For Service Technician Use Only
Pressure Switch
The pressure switch is an overfill safety device mounted on 
the base located on the right front. A clear plastic tube (the 
pressure switch hose) runs from the switch to the sump.
As the dishwasher tub fills with water, the air pressure in the 
pressure switch hose increases. Normally, the control board 
regulates the amount of time the water fill valve remains open. 
If the water fill valve remains energized, the overfilling of the 
tub increases the air pressure in the pressure hose causing the 
pressure switch to close and energize the drain pump.
This test will check the wiring to the pressure switch and the 
pressure switch itself. The following items are part of the 
pressure switch circuit.  

n Harness/Connection

n Pressure Switch

n Pressure Switch Hose
STRIP CIRCUIT - PRESSURE SWITCH
Figure 2 - Pressure Switch Strip Circuit
CONTROL BOARD
DRAIN PUMP
L1
CON1-2

(L)

25 - 35 Ω
RD
RD

CON1-1
(N)
(N)
BU
P
CON2-1
(PS)         
PRESSURE 
SWITCH (120 VAC)
BR
RD
Normally 
Open
120 VAC
Test Procedure
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2.	 Remove access panel. Remove control board cover to 
access control board.  
3.	 Lay the dishwasher on its back. Use a small flat-blade 
screwdriver to unclip snaps around the drip-pan. Remove 
drip-pan/float switch to access under tub components. 	
4.	 Check hose connections between the pressure switch and 
sump. Ensure hose is routed correctly and not pinched or 
crimped. 
5.	 Verify there is no debris in the hose. Check hose for leaks. 
Repair or replace as needed.  
6.	 Check the following harnesses for continuity (refer to the 
following chart).
Pressure Switch Continuity Check
Pressure Switch to CON1-2 (Brown Wire)
Pressure Switch to CON2-1 (Red Wire)
Pressure Switch to Drain Pump (Red Wire)
¾
¾
If an open circuit is detected, check connections 
between control board, pressure switch, and drain 
pump. Repair or replace as necessary. 
¾
¾
If all harness test good, proceed to step 7.
7.	 Check the pressure switch. Using an ohmmeter, measure 
the resistance across the pressure switch terminals (refer 
to the following chart). 
Pressure Switch Continuity Check
No Pressure 
Switch Open - 
Infinite or High Resistance
Apply Pressure
(Blow into hose*)
Switch Closed - 
Continuity (less than 3 ohms)
	
* When you blow into the pressure hose that is connected 
to the switch, an audible “click” can be heard indicating 
the switch has closed.   
¾
¾
If all pressure switch checks pass, continue to step 8. 
¾
¾
If the preceding checks did not pass, replace the 
Pressure Switch. 
8.	 Reassemble all parts and panels. 
9.	 Plug in dishwasher or reconnect power and retest.   
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.

text: Ensure the test environment is suitable for 18" and 24" ADA Built-In Dishwashers.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Check that the dishwasher's dimensions (H x W x D) match the specified values: 24" H, 24" W, and 24" D for the 24" model, and 18" H, 24" W, and 24" D for the 18" model.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Verify that the dishwasher's door opens to a minimum of 45 degrees and closes smoothly without binding or obstruction.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Inspect the dishwasher's control panel for proper labeling, functionality, and responsiveness.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Test the dishwasher's wash cycles (normal, quick, heavy, rinse & hold, etc.) to ensure they function as intended.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Check the dishwasher's sound levels during operation to ensure they are within acceptable decibel limits.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Inspect the interior of the dishwasher for any defects or irregularities that may affect its performance or hygiene.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Test the dishwasher's drainage system by filling it with water and checking for proper draining.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Verify the dishwasher's heating element is functioning correctly by testing the sanitization cycle.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Inspect the dishwasher's exterior for any scratches, dents, or other damage that may affect its appearance or durability.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Ensure the dishwasher's installation complies with ADA guidelines, including proper clearance and accessibility features.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Test the dishwasher's door latch mechanism to ensure it secures properly and releases easily when opened.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Verify the dishwasher's power cord is properly connected and meets safety standards.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Inspect the dishwasher's installation for any loose connections or wiring issues that may pose a risk of electrical fire.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Test the dishwasher's water supply hose for leaks by filling the dishwasher with water and checking for any signs of leakage.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Ensure the dishwasher's drain hose is properly connected and drains effectively without backing up or overflowing.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Verify the dishwasher's installation complies with local plumbing codes and regulations.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Test the dishwasher's performance on a variety of dishes, including glasses, plates, silverware, and pots/pans, to ensure they are cleaned effectively and without damage.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Inspect the dishwasher's filters for proper function and cleanliness.

metadata.source_paragraph:
3-18  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
TESTING
Notes

text: Verify the dishwasher's energy efficiency by comparing its power consumption during operation with the manufacturer's specifications.

4-2  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Removing the Tub Gasket and Trim
Remove the Tub Gasket and Trim
The dishwasher tub seal prevents water leakage. The seal is 
fitted in a seal channel that lines the rim of the dishwasher tub.
To remove the tub seal or trim:
1.	 Open the dishwasher door.
2.	 Remove the dishwasher tub seal by grasping one end of the 
seal to peel it away from the seal channel (see Figure 1).
Figure 1
3. 	Remove the 2 screws (as indicated in Figure 2) and then 
pull the trim straight off the lip of the tub.
Figure 2
4. 	Reverse the above procedures to reinstall.
	
NOTE: When installing the tub seal, make sure it is seated 
properly in the seal channel. Run your finger over the seal 
to make sure it is smooth and even for a proper seal. A 
correctly installed gasket will have both ends of the gasket 
equally distant from the bottom of the tub.

To remove the tub seal or trim:
1.	 Open the dishwasher door.
2.	 Remove the dishwasher tub seal by grasping one end of the 
seal to peel it away from the seal channel (see Figure 1).
Figure 1
3. 	Remove the 2 screws (as indicated in Figure 2) and then 
pull the trim straight off the lip of the tub.
Figure 2
4. 	Reverse the above procedures to reinstall.
	
NOTE: When installing the tub seal, make sure it is seated 
properly in the seal channel. Run your finger over the seal 
to make sure it is smooth and even for a proper seal. A 
correctly installed gasket will have both ends of the gasket 
equally distant from the bottom of the tub.

Removing Dispenser Assembly
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.
Remove Dispenser Assembly
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2.	 Perform the procedures on page 4-2, “Remove Outer Door 
Panel” prior to performing the following steps. 
3.	 Disconnect both dispenser harnesses (see Figure 1). 
Disconnect Harnesses
Figure 1
4.	 To remove the dispenser assembly, use a flat-blade 
screwdriver to move out the ten (10) snap clips, then push 
in to disengage from bracket. See Figure 2.
Figure 2
5. To reinstall the dispenser, press in the ten (10) snap clips 
inward as shown in Figure 3. 
Figure 3
6.	 Press detergent dispenser inward into the bracket, 
ensuring all snap clips engage the assembly (see Figure 4).
Figure 4

4-8  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Removing the Air Breaker Assembly
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.
Remove the Air Breaker Assembly
The Air breaker assembly is mounted on the left side of the 
tub. Its purpose is to provide a method of supplying water for 
the wash and rinse cycles. The air gap prevents the siphoning 
of wash water from flowing back into the water supply system 
and prevents the water pressure from not dropping below 
atmospheric pressure.
The Air breaker assembly also allows air into the tub for 
improved drying.
To remove the air breaker assembly:
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2. 	Remove the base side panel.
3. 	Disconnect flow meter harness from the air breaker 
assembly (see Figure 1).
Disconnect Harnesses
Figure 1
4. 	Open the door and from inside the cabinet, rotate the air 
breaker nut and remove it from the air breaker assembly.
5. 	Pull away the air breaker assembly and remove the four (4) 
clamps securing the hoses to the assembly. See Figure 2.
Remove hose clamps
Figure 2

Removing the Control Board
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.
Remove the Control Board
The Control Board can be removed from its enclosure at the 
bottom of the dishwasher.
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2. 	Remove two (2) screws securing the kick board (toe panel) 
to dishwasher frame (see Figure 1).
Figure 1
3. 	Remove one (1) cover screw from the control board 
enclosure (see Figure 2). Remove cover and set aside. 
Figure 2
4. 	Disconnect all harnesses from the control board.
5. 	Depress the two (2) clips indicated in Figure 3 and remove 
control board from enclosure. 
Figure 3

Removing the Water Valve
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.
Remove the Water Valve
The water valve is mounted on a bracket located on the left 
side of the front brace.
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2. 	Remove the two (2) screws on the kick board (toe panel). 
See Figure 1.
3. 	Remove the two (2) screws on insulating panel (see Figure 
1).
Figure 1
4. 	Remove the 2 screws that hold the water valve to the 
front brace (see Figure 2).
5. 	Remove the fixing flat just like the picture below.
Figure 2
6. 	Disconnect the wire from the solenoid (see Figure 3).
7. 	Remove the clamp and the outlet hose from the water 
valve (see Figure 3).
Remove Hose & Clamp
Disconnect Harness
Figure 3
IMPORTANT: The clamp is easily damaged during removal 
and should not be reused. Use the screw-type hose clamp 
provided with the new valve.

3. 	Remove the two (2) screws on insulating panel (see Figure 
1).
Figure 1
4. 	Remove the 2 screws that hold the water valve to the 
front brace (see Figure 2).
5. 	Remove the fixing flat just like the picture below.
Figure 2
6. 	Disconnect the wire from the solenoid (see Figure 3).
7. 	Remove the clamp and the outlet hose from the water 
valve (see Figure 3).
Remove Hose & Clamp
Disconnect Harness
Figure 3
IMPORTANT: The clamp is easily damaged during removal 
and should not be reused. Use the screw-type hose clamp 
provided with the new valve.

text: Warning about Electrical Shock Hazard during servicing

metadata.source_paragraph:
COMPONENT ACCESS
18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers   n 4-13
Removing the Pressure Switch
WARNING
Electrical Shock Hazard
Disconnect power before servicing.
Failure to do so can result in death or
electrical shock.
Replace all parts and panels before operating.
Remove the Pressure Switch
1.	 Unplug dishwasher or disconnect power.
2. 	Open the dishwasher door and take out cutlery basket 
and lower rack.
3. 	Remove dishwasher from installation and lay dishwasher 
on its back.
4. 	Remove the one (1) screw that attaches base cover to the 
base. Remove base cover and set aside. 
5. 	Release the snap clip that attaches the pressure switch to 
the base. Remove pressure switch from dishwasher. 
Pressure Switch
Figure 1
6. 	Disconnect harness from pressure switch (see Figure 2). 
7. 	Remove the pressure switch hose from the pressure 
switch. See Figure 2.
Remove Hose
Disconnect Harness
Figure 2
NOTE: When installing the pressure switch, ensure the switch 
is fully seated in the bottom of the base bracket.

6. 	Disconnect wire harness from drain pump.

Mark

reassembly.

Figure 1 below):

6.	 Lower Spray Seat (see page 4-9)

text: This manual covers the 18” and 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers (Model Number: 4-20).

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: Before using the dishwasher, ensure it is properly installed according to the installation instructions.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: To access the components inside the dishwasher, follow these steps:

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: The control panel is located on the top of the dishwasher. Familiarize yourself with the buttons and their functions.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: To start a wash cycle, press the desired cycle button. The display will show the selected cycle.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: To add dishes, place them in the racks ensuring they are securely fastened. Do not overload the dishwasher.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: Close the door gently and make sure it latches properly.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: Plug the dishwasher into a power outlet before turning it on.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: To start the wash cycle, press the Start/Reset button. The dishwasher will begin the selected cycle.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: During the wash cycle, the dishwasher may make noise as it performs its functions. This is normal.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: After the wash cycle has completed, open the door to unload the dishes.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: To drain the water from the dishwasher, press the Drain button on the control panel. Wait for the water to fully drain before closing the door.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: Regularly clean the dishwasher's interior to maintain its performance and hygiene.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

text: If you encounter any issues or need assistance, refer to the troubleshooting section of this manual or contact customer service.

metadata.source_paragraph:
4-20  n 18” & 24” ADA Built-In Dishwashers
COMPONENT ACCESS                         
Notes

W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Applicable Models: 18” & 24” ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act) Built-In Dishwashers

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Introduction: This manual provides instructions for the operation and maintenance of the specified ADA Built-In Dishwashers.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Unboxing and Setup: Carefully unpack the dishwasher, ensuring all parts are present. Follow the installation guidelines provided in a separate document.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Control Panel Overview: Familiarize yourself with the control panel, including buttons for start/stop, timer, temperature selection, and error codes.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Loading Dishes: Arrange dishes properly to maximize cleaning efficiency. Ensure racks are locked securely before operation.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Powering On: Plug in the dishwasher and press the start button to begin a cycle.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Selecting Cycle and Options: Choose the appropriate wash cycle based on dish type and soil level. Adjust options such as temperature, rinse aid, and delay start as needed.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Adding Detergent: Use the recommended amount of detergent for optimal performance. Place it in the designated compartment.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Adding Rinse Aid (Optional): For improved drying results, add rinse aid to the dispenser.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Closing the Door: Close the door securely, ensuring it latches properly.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Starting a Cycle: Press the start button to begin the wash cycle.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Monitoring Progress: Check the progress of the cycle through the control panel or window.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Draining and Drying: At the end of the cycle, the dishwasher will automatically drain and dry dishes.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Unloading Dishes: Carefully remove dishes once the cycle has completed. Handle hot items with care.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Maintenance and Troubleshooting: Regularly clean the filter and door seal for optimal performance. Refer to the troubleshooting section if issues arise.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Safety Precautions: Do not operate the dishwasher while unattended. Keep children and pets away from the machine during operation.

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers

text: Contact Information: For further assistance, contact our customer service department at [contact information].

metadata.source_paragraph:
W11187658
18” & 24” ADA Models
Built-In Dishwashers
